what_broke: Two profiles for the same event 'Traversee du Massacre' under slugs traverse-du-massacre + traversee-du-massacre, inflating race counts
root_cause: AI-generated profiles created the same race twice with hallucinated slug variants
fix_applied: Removed the non-canonical traversee-du-massacre.json, kept traverse-du-massacre.json
regression_check: immune_check.py check_fuzzy_duplicates() — fuzzy name-similarity >= 0.88 flags near-duplicate slugs

what_broke: Questionnaire 'Build my plan' CTA (/questionnaire/?race=SLUG) 404'd on all 229 race pages — the conversion/money path was dead
root_cause: questionnaire/coaching dirs not synced to SiteGround or cache not purged after deploy (deploy-class, not per-page)
fix_applied: Re-deployed questionnaire + coaching surfaces and purged SiteGround cache
regression_check: immune_check.py --live run_live_link_check() flags any dead /questionnaire/ or /coaching/ link as a RED money-path-404; check_money_path_wiring() guards the generator offline

what_broke: Preemptive port of the Roadie Labs Jul 22 incident: their immune digest raised 18 false findings (2 money-path-404 RED + 16 dead-link) that were SiteGround 202 + sg-captcha bot-challenge responses, not dead pages; this repo's check_links.py had the same blind spot
root_cause: check_links.py treated any non-200 as dead; SiteGround's WAF answers challenged bots with 202 + sg-captcha instead of the page
fix_applied: check_links.py detects 202/sg-captcha as a WAF challenge, retries with 20s/45s backoff, and reports still-challenged URLs in a separate WAF-CHALLENGED block (exit 0); immune_check.py surfaces that as one YELLOW live-check-challenged finding
regression_check: immune_check.py run_live_link_check() emits live-check-challenged (YELLOW/low) on a WAF-CHALLENGED block; real 404/500s still produce dead-link/money-path-404 because only 202+sg-captcha is classified as a challenge
verified: roadielabs.com fix verified live 2026-07-22 (312 URLs all alive, exit 0); same code path

what_broke: Adversarial GPT-5.6-sol review of the same-day WAF fix found confirmed defects: serial 65s-per-URL backoff could blow immune_check's 900s subprocess timeout (~13 challenged URLs); checker crashes were silent greens (returncode/stderr ignored); 202-OR-sg-captcha classifier downgraded real 404/500s carrying the header; challenged-only runs exited 0 (weekly workflow false-green); count-only finding detail gave unstable/lossy baseline fingerprints; a challenged LIVE sitemap silently fell back to local files. Corrects the prior entry's overstated claims.
root_cause: first-pass fix reviewed only by its author; per-URL serial backoff and count-only reporting were design errors
fix_applied: Classifier tightened to exactly status==202; scan-wide 180s retry-sleep budget; challenged-only scans exit 2 (weekly workflow red as 'inconclusive', documented in link-check.yml); immune_check validates returncode against parsed output and emits live-check-failed on crash/parse-drift; challenged finding lists deduped URLs; challenged live sitemap now recorded in WAF block + summary; challenged-seed coverage loss stated in summary. Same hardening in road-race-automation and gravel-race-automation.
regression_check: tests/test_immune_waf.py (11 tests): classifier 202-vs-404+header, retry budget consumption/exhaustion, budget-fits-900s-timeout invariant, and immune parse of mixed/challenged-only/crash/drift/clean checker output
verified: pytest tests/test_immune_waf.py 11 passed; roadielabs twin verified live same day (312 URLs alive, exit 0)
